evidence: "One of the Best and Most Important Documentaries I've Seen in a Long Time!"
"The film's social commentary is never insightful enough for it to work as a drama, and it's never funny or outrageous enough for it to work as satire either."
"The story was interesting for the first half of the film, but I felt like it really dragged around the halfway point and really wanted it to wrap things up."
"Even at a very short 73 minutes, it becomes apparent at the halfway point that the makers behind this struggled to find enough material to fill the runtime."
"The film runs close to 2 hours and there is about 15 minutes of footage that could have been left on the cutting room floor."
"The only real complaint I have about is that it doesn't go into a whole lot of detail about the NSA and what he was fighting against..."
"The writing is surprisingly bad too with more than a handful of awful jokes that become increasingly irritating to listen to."
"Martin Scorsese has once again brought us another masterpiece with one of the most fun and entertaining viewing experiences I've ever had!"
"If you are a fan of The Sex Pistols or Punk Rock in general, this documentary is a must watch!"
"...the director does an excellent job of crafting the film with a tone that's comparable to the works of David Fincher..."
"...the film is very much a Dramedy and does a good job of keeping the tone balanced delivering a fair dose of laughs along with dramatic tearjerking moments as well."
"...it's a truly inspiring (and important) true story and one of the years best films."
"...it's actually a very heartwarming and feel-good movie and has a pretty good message about letting go of the past."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for documentaries and comedies, particularly those that are well-crafted and emotionally resonant. They tend to favor films with high production quality but express disappointment when pacing is slow or narratives are overly simple.
